title: GH #3: Harden --open temp file writes against symlink clobbering
description: Imported from origin GitHub issue #3. mdo --open writes generated HTML to a deterministic path under the OS temp directory; if that path already exists as a symlink, fs::write follows it and overwrites the symlink target. Harden temp/cache output handling so same-machine attackers cannot clobber arbitrary user-writable files.
acceptance_criteria: Use a private per-user temp/cache directory with restrictive permissions where applicable; avoid following symlinks when opening the output path; add a Unix regression test proving a pre-created symlink target is not overwritten.
notes:
Source: https://github.com/maphew/mdo/issues/3
GitHub title: Harden --open temp file writes against symlink clobbering
close_reason: Implemented secure --open temp output handling and default Markdown HTML sanitization with regression tests

title: GH #2: Sanitize Markdown HTML output by default
description: Track implementation of GitHub issue #2. Rendered Markdown should sanitize unsafe raw HTML by default, provide an explicit unsafe opt-in only if needed, and include regression coverage for scripts and event-handler attributes.
acceptance_criteria: Generated HTML strips script tags and event-handler attributes by default; tests cover active-content sanitization; behavior is documented or surfaced through CLI help if an opt-in flag is added.
notes:
Source: https://github.com/maphew/mdo/issues/2
Reopened because GitHub #2 is still open and the sanitizer implementation exists only as uncommitted changes in ../mdo-gh2-sanitize, not on main/origin.
close_reason: Implemented secure --open temp output handling and default Markdown HTML sanitization with regression tests

title: Windows command injection via crafted .md filename in launch_browser (cmd /C start)
description:
HIGH-severity command injection found in the 2026-05-31 whole-project security review.

## Where
src/main.rs:215-223 — launch_browser(), Windows branch:

    std::process::Command::new("cmd")
        .args(["/C", "start", "", &path.to_string_lossy()])
        .spawn()?;

`path` is the temp output path built by temp_output_for() (src/main.rs:125-143), whose final filename component is taken verbatim from the input file's stem:

    Ok(source_dir.join(format!("{stem}.html")))

## Why it is exploitable (attacker-controlled input)
- mdo-open.exe is registered as the Windows Explorer "Open as HTML" handler for .md files (scripts/install-explorer.ps1:181 -> `"<exe>" "%1"`). Its entire purpose is opening .md files the user did NOT author (email attachments, downloads). The attacker chooses the filename. mdo-open forwards %1 to `mdo --open <file>` via Command (no shell), so the filename arrives intact.
- Windows filenames may legally contain the cmd metacharacters & ^ % ( ).
- Rust's std::process::Command only wraps an argument in quotes when it contains whitespace (or is empty); it does NOT escape `&`. The 1.77.2 "BatBadBut" fix only adds metacharacter escaping when the *program* is a .bat/.cmd file — invoking cmd.exe directly (as here) still uses plain whitespace-based quoting.
- private_temp_root() on Windows is just %TEMP%\mdo, so when the username has no space the entire output path has no space. The path arg is then emitted unquoted and `&` in the stem is interpreted by cmd.exe as a command separator.

## Proof of concept
Open (double-click via the installed handler, or run `mdo --open`) a file named:

    report&calc&.md

Output path becomes %TEMP%\mdo\<hash>\report&calc&.html and the spawned command line is:

    cmd /C start "" C:\...\report&calc&.html

cmd parses this as three commands: `start "" C:\...\report`  &  `calc`  &  `.html`  -> launches calc.exe. Substitute calc with any executable on PATH, a cmd builtin, or a %-expanded path. Opening a document executes an attacker-chosen command.

Note: the injected payload must contain no spaces (a space makes Rust quote the whole arg, neutralizing `&`), so fully argument-controlled RCE is constrained — but launching arbitrary no-space PATH/planted executables from opening a document is already a High-severity boundary crossing.

## Recommended fix (this bead)
Do not route the path through `cmd /C start`. On Windows, open the file with the Win32 ShellExecuteW(NULL, "open", path, NULL, NULL, SW_SHOWNORMAL) API — e.g. via the maintained `opener` or `open` crate — which takes the path as a single parameter and never goes through cmd command-line parsing. Keep the non-blocking, detached, no-console-flash behavior that mdo-open relies on. The macOS (`open`) and Linux (`xdg-open`) branches already pass the path as a single argv argument and are not affected.

## Acceptance criteria
- Windows `--open` no longer spawns cmd.exe; it uses ShellExecuteW (or an equivalent single-parameter open API / crate).
- Opening a file named `a&calc&.md` with `--open` does NOT launch calc.exe or any injected command.
- macOS and Linux open behavior unchanged; no console-window flash regression on Windows.
- A regression test (or a documented manual check) covers a filename containing cmd metacharacters.

## Design notes
Prefer a small, maintained crate (opener/open) or a direct windows-crate ShellExecuteW call over hand-rolled escaping. See the complementary defense-in-depth bead (sanitize the output filename stem in temp_output_for); either fix alone closes the PoC, both together is best.
notes:
GitHub issue: https://github.com/maphew/mdo/issues/7 . Defense-in-depth complement: mdo-67q (committed 15a47d0). Follow-up: mdo-3a9 (add Windows CI).

COMMITTED 2026-05-31 as 9748dbf 'Fix Windows command injection in launch_browser (use ShellExecuteW via opener)'.
- Cargo.toml: Windows-only dep opener = "0.8".
- src/main.rs launch_browser() Windows branch -> opener::open(path).map_err(io::Error::other)? (Win32 ShellExecuteW, no cmd; verified in opener 0.8.4 source). macOS/Linux branches unchanged.
- Verified on Linux: fmt --check, clippy -D warnings, test (10 pass), audit (exit 0).
- Windows compile NOT verified locally (no win toolchain); release.yml builds windows-latest so it is compile-checked at release; mdo-3a9 adds PR-time Windows CI.
- Commit is local only; branch is ahead of origin and NOT pushed. Left in_progress pending push + Windows compile verification.

title: Pin write-permission GitHub Actions to immutable SHAs
description: Security review finding: release.yml pins action references, but pages.yml and metrics.yml still use mutable tags while running with pages/id-token or contents:write permissions. A compromised or moved action tag could execute with publish privileges. Pin actions in write-token workflows to full commit SHAs and keep human-readable version comments for maintenance.
acceptance_criteria: pages.yml and metrics.yml actions are pinned to full commit SHAs or documented exceptions; write-token jobs keep least-privilege permissions; Dependabot/update process for pinned actions is documented.
close_reason: Implemented workflow hardening and dependency audit automation with local validation.

title: Bind manual release builds to the requested tag
description: Security review finding: .github/workflows/release.yml accepts a workflow_dispatch tag input and publishes to RELEASE_TAG, but the build jobs use the dispatch ref checkout rather than explicitly checking out that tag. A manual dispatch can therefore publish binaries built from a different ref under an existing tag, breaking release/source provenance. Update the workflow so all build and publish steps checkout/verify the selected tag, or remove manual tag dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: workflow_dispatch releases build from the exact input tag; the workflow verifies HEAD matches the tag before uploading assets; tag-push releases continue to work.
close_reason: Implemented workflow hardening and dependency audit automation with local validation.

title: GH #6: Finish GitHub release workflow for this fork
description: Imported from origin GitHub issue #6. A release workflow exists locally, but the open GitHub issue also requires least-privilege token permissions and pinned action references. Use this as the parent/coordination bead for closing the upstream GitHub release workflow issue after the hardening beads are done.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow exists under .github/workflows/; release publishing works from maphew/mdo; GITHUB_TOKEN permissions are least privilege with write access limited to the release job; third-party actions are pinned to immutable commit SHAs or exceptions are documented.
notes:
Source: https://github.com/maphew/mdo/issues/6
GitHub title: Add GitHub release workflow for this fork
Related completed local bead: mdo-p0q
close_reason: Closed linked GitHub issue #6 after verifying v0.3.0 release publication, release workflow success, least-privilege permissions, and pinned release actions.

title: GH #4: Narrow GitHub Actions token permissions to the release job
description: Imported from origin GitHub issue #4. The release workflow grants contents: write at workflow scope, so build matrix jobs receive a write-capable GITHUB_TOKEN while running tests/builds and dependency build scripts. Limit write permissions to the release publishing job.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow-level permissions default to contents: read; build jobs have read-only permissions; only the release publishing job has contents: write; release publishing still works with github.token.
notes:
Source: https://github.com/maphew/mdo/issues/4
GitHub title: Narrow GitHub Actions token permissions to the release job
close_reason: Completed release workflow permission narrowing and action SHA pinning

title: GH #5: Pin GitHub Actions to commit SHAs
description: Imported from origin GitHub issue #5. Release workflow actions use mutable references such as actions/checkout@v4, dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable, actions/upload-artifact@v4, and actions/download-artifact@v4. Pin action references to immutable commit SHAs or document exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: Release workflow third-party actions are pinned to full commit SHAs or have documented exceptions; comments preserve human-readable versions where useful; update automation is considered for future maintenance.
notes:
Source: https://github.com/maphew/mdo/issues/5
GitHub title: Pin GitHub Actions to commit SHAs
close_reason: Completed release workflow permission narrowing and action SHA pinning

title: Add a Windows compile/check job to CI (PR CI is ubuntu-only)
description:
CI does not compile the Windows code paths, so Windows-only breakage is caught late (only at release).

## Problem
.github/workflows/ci.yml runs a single job on `ubuntu-latest`. All of its steps (cargo fmt/clippy/test/audit/package) build for the Linux host only. Any code under `#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]` / `#[cfg(windows)]` and any `[target.'cfg(windows)'.dependencies]` are never compiled on PRs.

This was surfaced by the launch_browser command-injection fix (mdo-r5j / GitHub issue 7): it adds a Windows-only `opener` dependency and a Windows-only `opener::open(...)` call that the PR pipeline cannot compile. Today the only place that actually compiles the Windows branch is .github/workflows/release.yml (windows-latest / x86_64-pc-windows-msvc), which runs at release time — too late to catch a broken PR.

## Proposed change
Add a Windows job to ci.yml (windows-latest) that at minimum runs:

    cargo check --all-targets --all-features --locked

and ideally also `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo test`. This compiles the cfg(windows) paths and the Windows-only dependency tree (opener -> windows-sys), failing the PR if they don't build. A matrix over {ubuntu-latest, windows-latest} (and optionally macos-latest) on the existing check job is the cleanest form.

Note: a concurrent change is hardening GitHub workflow security (commit 80f70d1) and pinning actions to SHAs (mdo-9g6); coordinate so the new job follows the same pinning/permissions conventions.

## Acceptance criteria
- CI has a job running on windows-latest that compiles the crate's Windows cfg paths (cargo check or build, --locked) and fails the PR on a Windows compile error.
- The job exercises the Windows-only dependency (opener) so a future break in launch_browser's Windows branch is caught at PR time, not release time.
- Linux (and any existing) coverage is retained.

## Design notes
Keep it cheap: a `cargo check` on windows-latest is enough to close the compile-verification gap; full test execution on Windows is a nice-to-have. Reuse Swatinem/rust-cache and the same toolchain action already in ci.yml.
close_reason: Added a windows-latest CI job that runs cargo check --all-targets --all-features --locked.

title: Sanitize output filename stem in temp_output_for (defense in depth)
description:
Defense-in-depth hardening identified in the 2026-05-31 security review, complementing the launch_browser command-injection fix.

## Where
temp_output_for() (src/main.rs:125-143) derives the output filename from the input file stem verbatim:

    let stem = input.file_stem().and_then(|s| s.to_str()).unwrap_or("document");
    ...
    Ok(source_dir.join(format!("{stem}.html")))

## Why
An attacker-controlled filename stem flows into a filesystem path and (today) into the browser-launch argument on Windows. Even after launch_browser stops using cmd.exe, sanitizing the stem bounds the blast radius if any downstream code ever re-introduces a shell, and yields cleaner temp filenames.

Strip or percent-encode characters that are dangerous to shells/cmd and unusual in filenames — e.g. & ^ % ( ) ! " plus control characters — before building the temp path.

This is independent of and complementary to the primary fix (use ShellExecuteW). Either fix alone closes the reported PoC; doing both is best.

## Acceptance criteria
- temp_output_for() replaces or strips cmd/shell metacharacters in the derived stem before constructing the path.
- A stem like `a&calc&` produces a safe filename containing no `&`.
- Normal filenames (letters, digits, dashes, common unicode) remain readable.

## Design notes
Keep the stable per-source-path hashing behavior; only the human-readable `<stem>.html` suffix needs sanitizing. A small allowlist (or a targeted denylist of shell/cmd metacharacters + control chars) is sufficient.
notes: Tracked publicly in GitHub issue: https://github.com/maphew/mdo/issues/7 . Primary fix: mdo-r5j.
close_reason: Sanitized temp output filename stems and covered shell metacharacter handling with tests.
